goal: Job queue: execution engine with retry logic and failure recovery

acceptance_criteria.description: JobWorker struct polls for pending jobs via DequeueNext, executes them, and updates status on completion or failure
Retry logic: failed jobs with retry_count < max_retries transition to RETRYING with exponential backoff (base 30s, max 15min). Jobs exceeding max_retries transition to FAILED permanently.
Stale job recovery: on worker startup, any RUNNING jobs older than 30 minutes are reset to RETRYING (handles service restart mid-execution)
Job execution is type-dispatched: each JobType maps to a handler function. Unknown job types fail immediately with descriptive error.
WebSocket broadcasts job_started, job_completed, job_failed events with job_id and engagement_id
Graceful shutdown: worker finishes current job before stopping on SIGTERM. Does not pick up new jobs during shutdown.

critical: Worker runs in a goroutine managed by the main service — not a separate binary
Job execution sets app.current_user_id on the DB connection for RLS
Failed jobs record last_error with enough detail to diagnose (not just 'error')
No panic recovery that swallows errors silently — log and record the failure
high: Worker poll interval is configurable via env var (default 5s)
Exponential backoff uses jitter to prevent thundering herd on mass retry
Context cancellation propagated to job handlers for clean timeout
slog structured logging for all job lifecycle events (started, completed, failed, retrying)
medium: Metrics endpoint for job queue depth, avg execution time, failure rate
